title: hieracium australius
intro: Hieracium australius, also known as the Southern Hawkweed, is a type of flowering plant native to the mountainous regions of Australia and New Zealand. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating skin conditions such as eczema, acne, and wounds, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Hieracium australius include its ability to reduce inflammation, promote wound healing, and exhibit antioxidant properties. Herbal preparations of Hieracium australius can be made into teas, tinctures, and infusions, which are often used topically or taken orally to reap its benefits.
